before_code:
# /cleanup Phase-4 worker template (current behavior)
# Audit items 5 (NAMING), 6 (LINE PACKING), 12 (STRUCTURE) report status as one of:
#   * applied at L<n>
#   * n/a: <reason>
#   * deferred for Phase 5
#
# Worker dispatched on `m6_2_extra_rep_levelRaise_bridge` (244-line body) reports:
#   12. STRUCTURE: 219 lines after golf; sub-helpers not extracted because
#       the signature is locked and downstream consumer needs this single closed form
# Worker dispatched on `miyake_4_6_5_iterated_helper_general`:
#   5. NAMING: existing convention preserved  -- did not rename, no objection logged
#   6. LINE PACKING: ok  -- did not pack, hypotheses stayed one-per-line
# Both runs report Gates: pass and main session accepts them.
# Result: bad names survive, long proofs survive, hypothesis blocks stay sparse.
after_code:
# /cleanup Phase-4 worker template (proposed)
# Items 5/6/12 become HARD GATES, not audit items:
#
# === HARD GATES (run REJECTED if any fail) ===
#   structure_gate:     body line count <= 60 (or <=15 for main theorems).
#                       Note: extracting `private` helpers ABOVE the declaration
#                       does NOT change the declaration's signature.
#                       "Signature locked" is not a valid reason to skip extraction.
#   naming_gate:        no forbidden patterns in declaration name:
#                       no `\d+_\d+_\d+_` (e.g. `4_6_5_`), no `m\d+_`,
#                       no `multipass_`, no `_aux\d+` numeric suffix.
#                       Rename per mathlib convention: describe what is PROVED.
#   line_packing_gate:  hypothesis lines packed to ~100 chars where possible.
#                       Multiple hypotheses per line is the norm, not the exception.
#
# === Audit-only items (report and address, deferral allowed) ===
#   1 (LINT), 3 (SET_OPTION), 4 (SIMP SQUEEZE), 7 (BY PLACEMENT), 8 (FORMAT),
#   9 (COMMENTS), 10 (DOCSTRING), 11 (VISIBILITY), 14 (JUNK DEF), 15 (EXISTS LEMMA),
#   16 (SHOW vs CHANGE), 17 (PUSH_NEG), 18 (GENERALISE)
#
# If body > 60 after worker run: REJECTED, re-dispatch with body line count as feedback.
# If declaration name still matches forbidden patterns: REJECTED, re-dispatch.
# If first hypothesis-rich line is below ~70 chars when next-token would fit:
#   REJECTED, re-dispatch with explicit pack instructions.
description: The /cleanup Phase-4 worker template has an escape valve that prevents quality enforcement on large files. Audit items 5 (NAMING), 6 (LINE PACKING), and 12 (STRUCTURE) are framed as report-or-defer, not pass-fail gates. On small files this is invisible (workers fix because there's little to defer); on large files (1000+ lines, many specialised declarations) workers routinely defer, leaving bad names, long proofs, and sparse hypothesis blocks in place. Specific failure modes observed: (1) 'signature locked' misread — workers refuse to extract `private` helpers ABOVE the declaration, claiming the signature-protection rule blocks it; the rule only blocks changing the declaration's OWN signature. (2) NAMING audit reports 'existing convention preserved' without ever proposing a rename, even when names encode internal scheme numbers (4_6_5_, m6_2_, multipass_) instead of mathematical content. (3) LINE PACKING reports 'ok' without packing one-per-line hypothesis blocks that could fit on shared lines up to 100 chars. The fix: convert items 5, 6, 12 to hard pass-fail gates with explicit rejection criteria. Re-dispatch workers whose runs violate any gate, feeding the violation back as the worker's prompt. Disambiguate 'signature locked' to clarify that helpers placed above the declaration are not signature changes. This was observed in a /cleanup run on an 8286-line modular forms file across ~15 sequential workers, where structure/naming/packing defects accumulated visibly while every worker reported 'Gates: pass'.
